title: creating container in network with API fails: invalid config provided: networks and static ip/mac address can only be used with Bridge mode networking
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

It does not seem possible to create a user container in a user network with the API.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

As user:

1. `podman network create n1`

This works, and creates a bridge, visible in `podman network ls`.

2. `podman pull quay.io/libpod/busybox`

3. `curl -v --unix-socket $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock --json '{"image": "quay.io/libpod/busybox", "name": "c1", "command": ["sleep", "infinity"], "remove": true, "Networks": {"n1": {}}}' http://nothing/v1.12/libpod/containers/create`

**Describe the results you received:**

Fails with

```
< HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error
{"cause":"networks and static ip/mac address can only be used with Bridge mode networking","message":"invalid config provided: networks and static ip/mac address can only be used with Bridge mode networking","response":500}
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

(1) this is a bridge mode networking (even though I expect this to be somehow be an emulated stack, as it's all as user). (2) I am not giving any "static IP/mac" here.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I think this is just the API getting confused. If I do this on the CLI:

    podman create --name c1 --network n1 quay.io/libpod/busybox

it works:

    45a6146aec8c  quay.io/libpod/busybox:latest      sh                    2 seconds ago   Created                        c1

It also works if I do this with system containers. Run the `pull` and `network create` with `sudo`, and try:

    sudo curl -v --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock --json '{"image": "quay.io/libpod/busybox", "name": "c1", "command": ["sleep", "infinity"], "remove": true, "Networks": {"n1": {}}}' http://nothing/v1.12/libpod/containers/create


**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18.4
Built:        Thu Aug 11 16:42:17 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
You still have to set the network mode to bridge in the spec.
It would properly make sense to automatically change this to bridge when the network mode is unset but networks are given.

I tried to use the deprecated `cni_networks` option:
```
curl -v --unix-socket $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock --json '{"image": "quay.io/libpod/busybox", "name": "c1", "command": ["sleep", "infinity"], "remove": true, "cni_networks": ["n1"]}' http://nothing/v1.12/libpod/containers/create
```

That does not fail, but the container has a "10.0.2.100" IP and no internal pod DNS, which smells like a slirp network. In the `podman create` reproducer I get inet 10.89.1.9 and can reach c1.dns.podman, which is what I expect from a bridge-type network.

@Luap99 : That sounds promising! How would I do that? https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#tag/containers/operation/ContainerCreateLibpod does not document how I'd set a network mode in the /create API, just in the "play kubernetes yaml" section.

This still smells wrong: (1) why does this work as root? and (2) there is no choice about this value. If I specify an existing network, that has a type. If you'd set anything else than the type, it would/should fail anyway, right?

> why does this work as root?

because network mode bridge is the default for root, rootless uses slirp4netns

> there is no choice about this value.

WDYM? you have to set the network mode as `"netns": { "nsmode": "bridge"}`, other options are host or none for example.

Thanks @Luap! Indeed this works! :tada: 

    curl -v --unix-socket $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock --json '{"image": "quay.io/libpod/busybox", "name": "c1", "command": ["sleep", "infinity"], "remove": true, "netns": { "nsmode": "bridge" }, "Networks": {"n1": {}}}' http://nothing/v1.12/libpod/containers/create

> there is no choice about this value.

I meant that the "n1" network that I attach the container to defines the network mode already, no? I might misunderstand that.
